summary: Moved a substantial first slice of map/platform collision (`mpcoll`) from assembly into C by adding `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c` and `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.h`, wiring the new C object into `obj_files.mk`, and deleting a large chunk of matching text from `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s`. The PR also promoted `CollData` from a sparse placeholder in `include/common_structs.h` to a more structured definition in `src/melee/ft/fighter.h`, then updated fighter, item, walljump, Ness PK Thunder/Yo-Yo, and other callers to use the new nested ECB and surface-field names. Review evidence is light: one collaborator approval said the slice looked good and that future `mpcoll` work could continue in a new PR.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c` with 1204 lines of C for the early `mpcoll` function range, including `func_80041C78`, `func_80041C8C`, `func_80041DD0`, `func_80041EE4`, `func_80042078`, `func_8004220C`, and later helpers through the visible `func_80043C6C` range.
Removed about 2674 lines from `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s`, leaving the C object to provide the matched functions while keeping remaining unmatched assembly in the build.
Added a minimal `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.h` that includes `global.h`, `types.h`, and `melee/ft/fighter.h`.

Deleted the old sparse `CollData` placeholder from `include/common_structs.h` and introduced a richer `CollData` layout in `src/melee/ft/fighter.h` with `ftECB`, `ECBFlagStruct`, and `SurfaceData` helper structs.

Renamed many direct collision offsets to structured fields, for example `x154_groundNormal` became `x14C_ground.normal`, `x160_rightwall_index` became `x160_rightwall.index`, `x174_leftwall_index` became `x174_leftwall.index`, and old ECB point offsets became `xA4_ecbCurrCorrect.left/right/top/bottom`.
Updated consumers in fighter, item, walljump, Ness, Fox, Mario, Luigi, Mewtwo, Zelda, and common fighter code to compile against the new `CollData` shape.

Added or preserved matching-oriented comments in new C, including decomp.me scratch links and notes such as `TODO: proper signatures, mplib.h`, `TODO: float order hack`, and uncertainty about a likely substruct at `CollData.x104`.
smart_moves: Kept the PR as an incremental `mpcoll` slice: only part of `mpcoll.s` was removed, and the reviewer explicitly suggested future `mpcoll` work continue in a new PR.
Introduced named helper structs (`ftECB`, `ECBFlagStruct`, `SurfaceData`) instead of continuing to spread raw `CollData` offsets through every caller.
Grouped related surface data as `index`, `unk`, and `normal`, which made call sites more self-documenting without pretending all fields were fully understood.
Used nested ECB names like `xA4_ecbCurrCorrect.left.x` and `x14C_ground.normal.y`, preserving offset-based prefixes while improving semantic readability.
Kept uncertainty visible in comments rather than over-naming unknown fields, e.g. marking `x104` as a suspected substruct and leaving several external `mplib` signatures as TODOs.

Used small inline helpers in matching-sensitive code, such as `clamp_above`, `clamp_below`, `update_min_max`, `Vec2_Interpolate`, and Ness Yo-Yo `push_ecb`, to express repeated assembly patterns while preserving match behavior.
Carried original debug strings and assertion behavior into C for `mpCollPrev`, including `OSReport` paths, line numbers, and `__assert("mpcoll.c", 228, "0")`.
decomp_lessons: For large shared structs, moving from filler fields to nested semantic substructures can unlock many downstream matches, but should retain offset prefixes until names are certain.
When a collision struct is used across fighter, item, and map systems, expect a decomp slice to require broad call-site churn even if the primary target is one assembly file.
Partially known fields should be named by observed role (`ground`, `rightwall`, `leftwall`, `ceiling`, `normal`, `index`) and left as `unk` where evidence is insufficient.

Matching `mpcoll` required preserving low-level float constants and ordering, evidenced by comments like `TODO: float order hack` and constants such as `lbl_804D7F9C = -FLT_MAX` and `lbl_804D7FA0 = FLT_MAX`.
Inline helper functions are useful for matching repeated min/max and state-push logic, but comments should document when an inline is inferred rather than confirmed.
Adding the C object before the remaining asm object in `obj_files.mk` is the standard pattern for gradually replacing assembly functions with matched C.
naming_conventions: Struct fields keep offset-prefixed names while adding semantics: `x14C_ground`, `x160_rightwall`, `x174_leftwall`, `x188_ceiling`.
Surface data fields use `.index`, `.unk`, and `.normal` under a `SurfaceData` wrapper.
ECB point data is expressed as `ftECB` with `.top`, `.bottom`, `.right`, and `.left` `Vec2` members.
Collision ECB snapshots are named by offset and role, including `x64_ecb`, `x84_ecb`, `xA4_ecbCurrCorrect`, `xC4_ecb`, and `xE4_ecb`.
Bitfield wrapper `ECBFlagStruct` exposes both `.bits` and `.raw`, with bit names still provisional (`b0`, `b1234`, `b5`, `b6`, `b7`).
Function symbols remain address-style `func_8004xxxx` names because this PR did not establish final names for most `mpcoll` routines.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced matched assembly functions by adding C implementations and deleting corresponding blocks from `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s` rather than rewriting the whole file at once.
Preserved decomp.me scratch URLs beside many new functions to document match provenance.
Used explicit constants and globals (`lbl_804D64A0`, `lbl_804D64A4`, `lbl_804D64A8`, `lbl_804D64AC`, `lbl_804D7F9C`, `lbl_804D7FA0`) when final variable names or placement were not yet known.
Used inlines for clamp/min/max behavior and vector interpolation to steer generated code toward the original assembly.
Matched debug/error paths in `func_80041C8C` with original `OSReport` strings, `g_debugLevel` checks, and `__assert` calls.
Left unknown external `mplib` calls as provisional declarations in `mpcoll.c` with a TODO for proper signatures.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.impact: Indicates the PR was synchronized with the author's upstream/work-in-progress branch, but gives no technical review details.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Looks good to me. Future work on `mpcoll` can be done in a new PR imo.
review_feedback.impact: Approved the incremental slice and explicitly encouraged splitting additional `mpcoll` work into later PRs.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue `mpcoll` after PR #457.
Search for subsequent renames of `CollData` fields such as `x104`, `x34_flags`, `x35_flags`, and `SurfaceData.unk`.
Look for PRs that move `CollData` out of `fighter.h` or add a dedicated collision header.
Compare later `mpcoll.c` revisions for final names of `func_80041EE4`, `func_80042078`, and `func_8004730C`.
Search for future review comments mentioning `TODO: float order hack` or the `-FLT_MAX` / `FLT_MAX` constants.
